mission.title: external-secrets: [AWS] Support the new EKS Pod Identity Agent
mission.description: [AWS] Support the new EKS Pod Identity Agent. Users encounter: "(probably from the AWS SDK):".

steps.title: Identify external-secrets troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your external-secrets deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=external-secrets
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `(probably from the AWS SDK):`

steps.title: Check current Pod configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets resources:
```bash
kubectl get pod -A
kubectl describe pod <name> -n <namespace>
```
## Description of the bug

External Secrets v0.9.9 installed in EKS does not work with the new [EKS Pod Identity Agent](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/pod-identities.html) feature (a

steps.title: Apply the fix for [AWS] Support the new EKS Pod Identity Agent
steps.description:

You can use `serviceAccountRef` only with IRSA, not Pod Identity. That's because the Pod Identity agent verifies the identity of the caller (ESO) an
```yaml
2023/12/13 09:44:17 Ignoring, HTTP credential provider invalid endpoint host, "169.254.170.23", only loopback hosts are allowed. <nil>
```

steps.title: Confirm [AWS] Support the new EKS Pod Identity Agent is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `(probably from the AWS SDK):` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: > Are you saying that this won't work because the pod has already assumed the role using the identity association, and can't then assume another role?

You can use `serviceAccountRef` only with IRSA, not Pod Identity. That's because the Pod Identity agent verifies the identity of the caller (ESO) and hence it can not impersonate a different service account.

> I'm unsure how the alternative soluti. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets: 2023/12/13 09:44:17 Ignoring, HTTP credential provider invalid endpoint host, "169.254.170.23", only loopback hosts are allowed. <nil>

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
